records.ety: [L. cogens, p. pr. of cogere to drive together, to force; co- + agere to drive. See Agent, a., and cf. Coact to force, Coagulate, p. a.]

defs: Compelling, in a physical sense; powerful.
Having the power to compel conviction or move the will; constraining; conclusive; forcible; powerful; not easily resisted.
quotes: No better nor more cogent reason.
Proofs of the most cogent description.
The tongue whose strains were cogent as commands, Revered at home, and felt in foreign lands.
